# Transcrição: 5. Lack Variety in Reasons to Call

- **Fonte:** 5. Lack Variety in Reasons to Call
- **Duração:** 01:41
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:41]

Lacking variety and reasons to follow up is a mistake in follow up. Just like in closing a transaction, you can't ask the customer the same thing over and over and over again. Not unlike you would not do the same thing with your kid every day before they get bored. You lack variety is a mistake in following people up. You need lots of variety. You can't be dependent upon one mechanism. It doesn't matter if you like handwritten notes or not. It doesn't matter whether you like phone calls. It doesn't matter whether you like leaving a message. This is for them, not for you. Because you lack variety, you become predictable and you run out of reasons to follow people up. So I'm going to use the whole gamut. Everything. Everything in my arsenal I will use. None of them do I like more than the other. You know which one I like the best? The one that works. I'm going to text. I'm going to email. I'm going to send a handwritten note. I'm going to make a personal visit. I hate personal visits. I hate actually getting out of a car, driving across town, walking in. But personal visits are so powerful. I mean you've got to make that investment. I hate getting on a plane and flying across country, going to see people. But it's a powerful investment. You have to be willing to do the things you don't want to do and use the full variety. Okay? Because that's what is going to show your client that you're there for the long run. That you have creativity. That you have solutions. That you're outside the band. That you're willing to do whatever it takes. Whenever it takes. However it takes to get the job done. Use variety. It's a big mistake. You get too comfortable, if you will, too reasonable with the same old mechanisms. Add variety to your reasons to follow up.
